title: Mediation - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Mediation 19 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Divorce/Custody/Support Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY BASIC CENTER BEND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline, short and long term shelter, case management and advocacy, crisis intervention, short term counseling, and mediation services between youth and their families. 2125 NE Daggett Lane Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Runaway and at-risk youth ages 11-17 Hours: Shelter and Crisis Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Other service times may vary. Email (541) 382-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION FAMILY MEDIATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mediation and conflict coaching services for disputes involving families, including parent-youth issues, elder issues, sibling issues, domestic relations, financial-only dissolutions, and parenting plans. Also provides specialized programs for mediation with foster families. 1400 Cross Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Mediation can be conducted outside of these hours as needed. Email (541) 344-5366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION MEDIATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mediation and conflict coaching services for disputes involving community members, interpersonal issues, landlords and tenants, property, noise, pets, workplace conflicts, consumers and merchants, businesses and clients, business dissolution, police/citizen dialogue, contract claims, and foreclosure avoidance. Assists county circuit courts by mediating small claims and residential eviction matters. 1400 Cross Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Appointments can be made outside of these hours. Email (541) 344-5366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION COOS COUNTY MEDIATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mediation and conflict coaching services for disputes involving community members, interpersonal issues, landlords and tenants, property, noise, pets, workplace conflicts, consumers and merchants, businesses and clients, business dissolution, police/citizen dialogue, contract claims, and foreclosure avoidance. Assists county circuit courts by mediating small claims and residential eviction matters. 1611 Virginia Avenue Suite 209 North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Appointments can be made outside of these hours. Email (458) 239-5435 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION DOUGLAS COUNTY MEDIATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mediation and conflict coaching services for disputes involving community members, interpersonal issues, landlords and tenants, property, noise, pets, workplace conflicts, consumers and merchants, businesses and clients, business dissolution, police/citizen dialogue, contract claims, and foreclosure avoidance. Assists county circuit courts by mediating small claims and residential eviction matters. 1000 W Stanton Avenue Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Appointments can be made outside of these hours. Email (458) 240-6536 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF BEAVERTON CENTER FOR MEDIATION AND DIALOGUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential mediation, facilitation, and conflict resolution education. Types of mediation include: Community Mediation: Conflicts involving noise, fences, parking, vandalism, animal complaints, property damage or maintenance, harassment, minor assaults, and conflicts over money and personal property. Foreclosure Avoidance Mediation: Utilizes specially trained facilitators to conduct face-to-face resolution conferences between borrowers and lenders to avoid home foreclosure. Housing Mediation: For tenants, landlords/housing providers wishing to discuss issues related to rent, repairs, communication, or any other issues or concerns related to the living arrangement. Manufactured Home (MFH) Mediation: Assists homeowners, residents, managers, and park owners to resolve conflicts associated with the parks and living situations. Home Sharing Mediation: Connects homeless high school students who are committed to graduating from high school with people willing to share their homes. Mediators facilitate rental contracts and a house rules discussion to provide a space for open communication and mutual expectation setting before move-in. Mediators check in with the parties after a few months to continue promoting open communication, contract modifications, and conflict resolution strategies as needed. Restorative Dialogue: Offers trained facilitators to help juvenile offenders and crime victims meet face-to-face. The dialogue is an opportunity to discuss the impacts of the harm, meaningful accountability, and make agreements to repair the harm. Takes most cases, with the exceptions of parenting plan, divorce cases, or court-ordered small claims, which are handled through Washington County. 12725 SW Millikan Way Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (503) 526-2523 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF GRESHAM EAST COUNTY RESOLUTIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists people to communicate, problem solving, and find solutions to their conflicts. Serves neighbors, businesses, landlords, and tenants. Workshops and courses in mediation and conflict communication are provided. Facilitators are provided for group issues. 1333 NW Eastman Parkway Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, weekends and evenings by appointment Email (503) 618-3247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY RESOLUTION SERVICES FAMILY LAW MEDIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides family law mediation for issues such as divorce, separation, custody, child support, spousal support, and finance and property division. Also offers a general family law clinic. Confidential Address Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Office hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5:30pm. Closed on holidays. Classes: Tuesday 9:30am-1pm, Wednesday 5:30pm-9pm Email (503) 655-8415 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY RESOLUTION SERVICES COMMUNITY DISPUTE RESOLUTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mediation services for various community disputes, including neighbor disputes, landlord/tenant issues, manufactured home community issues, consumer/merchant disputes, family relationships (including elder care issues), small claims, and workplace conflict. Services are voluntary and confidential. Confidential Address Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-1:30pm and 2:30pm-5:30pm. Closed on holidays. Email (503) 655-8850 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES FOR POLK COUNTY COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Trained mediators assist in finding solutions to conflicts. Mediators have experience with interpersonal disputes, family mediation (e.g., parent/teen, runaways), parenting plan adjustments, neighborhood disputes (e.g., barking dogs, property line/easement disputes), landlord/tenant disputes, institutional/business mediation, and victim/offender mediation. Also holds workshops, public lectures, and discussions on mediation and coaches individuals in conflicted situations. Meetings may be held in the client's community to ease time or transportation limitations. Does not mediate domestic violence cases. 320 SE Fir Villa Road Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Must be a resident of or employed in Polk County Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Special arrangements outside these hours may be possible. Email (503) 623-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SOLUTIONS OF CENTRAL OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps resolve conflicts and disagreements through community mediation. Mediators help parties communicate with understanding, explore solutions, and solve problems. Conflicts can include neighbor relationships, homeowner association issues, workplace problems, consumer/business disputes, family relationships (including separation/divorce or children's issues), money issues, landlord/tenant disputes, and land use disputes. Mediation is voluntary, cooperative, and confidential, and mediated by one or two impartial mediators who do not take sides, give legal advice, or impose settlements. Provides mediators for the Deschutes County Justice Court Small Claims Court. Litigants have the opportunity to resolve disputes through mediation before proceeding to trial before a judge. Provides mediator training, workshops, and facilitation services for businesses, government agencies, and nonprofit organizations. 1010 NW 14th Street Suite 100 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-4pm, and by appointment as needed Email (541) 383-0187 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DIRECTION SERVICE NATIONAL CENTER FOR APPROPRIATE DISPUTE RESOLUTION IN SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meeting facilitation, and other collaborative processes to resolve disagreements between parents and schools regarding children's educational programs and support services. Facilitates a variety of technical assistance services including training, workshops, and presentations, data analysis, systems improvement, and resource development. 1144 Gateway Loop Suite 200 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 359-4210 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON MEDIATION CENTER MEDIATION AND RESTORATIVE JUSTICE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Dispute resolution center offering mediation services through trained volunteer mediators. Mediates small claims, neighbor-to-neighbor disputes, harassment claims, vandalism, property conflicts, family relationships, animal disputes, employee disputes, landlord/tenant disputes, student disputes, noise issues, debt, youth disputes (including parent/teen conflict), and parking disputes. Also offers restorative justice conferencing for victims, allowing them to confront their offenders in a restorative setting. 1007 4th Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Phone hours: Monday-Friday 7am-8pm Email (541) 786-0270 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY MEDIATION & RESTORATIVE SERVICES LANE COUNTY FAMILY MEDIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers court-required parenting class for separating and divorcing parents, a confidential mediation process in which parents meet with a professional, court-approved mediator to develop a parenting plan that is in the best interests of the children, a Family Check-Up program, supervised parenting, and custody evaluations. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 682-4709 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINCOLN COMMUNITY DISPUTE RESOLUTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Dispute resolution and agreement development in the following areas: Housing, Workplace, Neighbors, Family Members, Youth, Businesses, and Government. Also offers Conflict Resolution training and group meeting facilitation with emphasis on consensus-building. 111 SE Douglas Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: By appointment only. Services are scheduled to accommodate needs of client Email (541) 574-9846 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR MEDIATION NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR BENTON AND LINN COUNTIES MEDIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mediation through a voluntary and confidential process where people with disputes come together, sit down with a neutral third-party mediator, and discuss ways to solve the dispute. Can assist with landlord/tenant disputes, and a juvenile victim/offender mediation program is also available. Offers periodic Small Claims Mediation Trainings for mediators and those committing to volunteer at small claims court mediations. 308 Broadalbin Street SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-2pm Email (503) 585-0651 6 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR MEDIATION NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR MARION COUNTY MEDIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mediation through a voluntary and confidential process where people with disputes come together, sit down with a neutral third-party mediator, and discuss ways to solve the dispute. Can assist with landlord/tenant disputes. A juvenile victim/offender mediation program is also available. Offers periodic Small Claims Mediation Trainings for mediators and those committing to volunteer at small claims court mediations. 347 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-2pm Email (503) 585-0651 5 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR MEDIATION NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR YAMHILL COUNTY MEDIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mediation through a voluntary and confidential process where people with disputes come together, sit down with a neutral third-party mediator, and discuss ways to solve the dispute. Can assist with landlord/tenant disputes. A juvenile victim/offender mediation program is also available. Offers periodic Small Claims Mediation Trainings for mediators and those committing to volunteer at small claims court mediations. 237 NE Ford Street Suite 6 McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-2pm Email (503) 585-0651 201 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RESOLUTIONS NORTHWEST COMMUNITY MEDIATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps community members resolve differences outside of the legal system. The program offers confidential and effective mediation services for families and youth, peer mediation training for youth in Portland Public Schools (PPS) and any other conflicts within Multnomah County. Includes a specific mediation program for landlords and tenants experiencing conflicts such as rent increases, rent past due, housing discrimination, noise, and accommodation needs that could otherwise lead to eviction. 4531 SE Belmont Street Suite 120 Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, evening and weekend hours by appointment Email (503) 595-4890 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RESOLVE CENTER FOR DISPUTE RESOLUTION AND RESTORATIVE JUSTICE MEDIATION AND FACILITATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides various mediation and facilitation services, including free landlord/tenant mediation and conflict coaching, community mediation, workplace interventions, and small claims and eviction mediation through Jackson County Circuit Court. 201 West Main Street Suite 3A Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Resident of Jackson or Josephine County Hours: Scheduled as needed Email (541) 770-2468 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY JUVENILE DEPARTMENT CONCILIATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides court-mandated and voluntary mediation services to assist couples in reaching an agreement about custody and parenting time before a court hearing. Also offers co-parenting education and counseling. 222 N 1st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: -Court mandated Mediation and Parent Education: Parties with minor children who have a pending domestic relations case in Washington County -Voluntary Mediation: Parties with minor children who do not have a pending domestic relations case in Washington County -All other services: Parties with minor children Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-3428 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EMERGENCY SERVICES CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION FAMILY MEDIATION SERVICES CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION MEDIATION SERVICES CENTER FOR DIALOGUE AND RESOLUTION MEDIATION SERVICES CITY OF BEAVERTON CENTER FOR MEDIATION AND DIALOGUE CITY OF GRESHAM EAST COUNTY RESOLUTIONS COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES FOR POLK COUNTY COMMUNITY MEDIATION SERVICES COMMUNITY SOLUTIONS OF CENTRAL OREGON COMMUNITY SOLUTIONS OF CENTRAL OREGON DIRECTION SERVICE NATIONAL CENTER FOR APPROPRIATE DISPUTE RESOLUTION IN SPECIAL EDUCATION EASTERN OREGON MEDIATION CENTER MEDIATION AND RESTORATIVE JUSTICE SERVICES LINCOLN COMMUNITY DISPUTE RESOLUTION LINCOLN COMMUNITY DISPUTE RESOLUTION NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR MEDIATION MEDIATION NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR MEDIATION MEDIATION NEIGHBOR-TO-NEIGHBOR MEDIATION MEDIATION RESOLUTIONS NORTHWEST COMMUNITY MEDIATION PROGRAM RESOLVE CENTER FOR DISPUTE RESOLUTION AND RESTORATIVE JUSTICE MEDIATION AND FACILITATION WASHINGTON COUNTY JUVENILE DEPARTMENT CONCILIATION SERVICES
